baseline.reason: Gets the vulnerability type (double free) and function (dialog_changed) correct, but the root cause mechanism is wrong. Claims buf_set_name frees pointers and dialog_changed frees them again, but buf_set_name replaces freed pointers with new allocations (line 487). The actual double free is between b_fname and b_sfname aliasing to the same allocation, not cross-function stale-pointer reuse.

vastdb.reason: Correctly traces the entire trigger path: empty buffer name -> buf_set_name -> fname_expand at lines 513-514 setting *sfname=*ffname -> buf->b_fname = buf->b_sfname creating alias -> write failure -> VIM_CLEAR(buf->b_fname) then VIM_CLEAR(buf->b_sfname) on same allocation = double free. Accurate and consistent with the actual patched diff and CVE-2024-41965.
